journal_articles.title: Reassessing Old Kingdom Tomb Architecture at Giza
journal_articles.subtitle: Spatial Organization and Elite Memory
journal_articles.abstract: This article reexamines the architecture of Old Kingdom mastaba tombs at Giza with a focus on spatial organization, access control, and the role of decorated spaces in the construction of elite memory. Using newly digitized survey data and 3D models, it demonstrates that tomb layout correlates closely with kinship networks and evolving mortuary cult practices.

journal_articles.title: New Discoveries in Old Kingdom Tombs at Saqqara
journal_articles.subtitle: Results of the 2017–2019 Seasons
journal_articles.abstract: Presenting the results of three excavation seasons at North Saqqara, this study reports on newly uncovered Old Kingdom tombs belonging to middle-ranking officials. The article details their architectural features, inscriptions, and material culture, and discusses implications for understanding provincial administration and mortuary practice.

journal_articles.title: Iconography and Identity in Old Kingdom Tomb Chapels
journal_articles.subtitle: A Quantitative Study of Tomb Decoration Programs
journal_articles.abstract: Using a database of 220 decorated Old Kingdom tomb chapels, this article applies quantitative methods to examine correlations between iconographic themes, titles, and tomb locations. It argues that visual programs were carefully calibrated to communicate social identity and local ritual expectations.

journal_articles.title: Rock-Cut Tombs of the Late Old Kingdom in Upper Egypt
journal_articles.abstract: This contribution surveys late Old Kingdom rock-cut tombs in Upper Egypt, focusing on regional variation and local workshop practices. Architectural documentation and stylistic analysis support a revised chronological framework for these monuments.

journal_articles.title: Recent Approaches to Teaching Middle Egyptian Grammar
journal_articles.subtitle: Blended Learning in the Egyptology Classroom
journal_articles.abstract: This article evaluates blended learning strategies for teaching Middle Egyptian grammar at the university level. Based on classroom experiments over three semesters, it assesses the effectiveness of digital exercises and automated feedback in improving student outcomes.

journal_articles.title: Building a Digital Corpus for Coptic Language Studies
journal_articles.subtitle: Methodological Reflections and First Results
journal_articles.abstract: The article presents the design and implementation of a new open-access digital corpus of Coptic texts. It discusses challenges related to lemmatization, part-of-speech tagging, and encoding diacritics, and demonstrates potential applications for historical linguistics and philology.

journal_articles.title: Temple Ritual and Royal Ideology in the Early New Kingdom
journal_articles.subtitle: Reconsidering the Karnak Daily Ritual Texts
journal_articles.abstract: By reexamining the daily ritual texts from Karnak, this article explores how early New Kingdom kings embedded royal ideology within temple liturgy. It highlights shifts in emphasis on divine kingship and cosmic order during the reigns of Ahmose and Thutmose III.

journal_articles.title: The Development of Amun Cults from the Middle Kingdom to the Ramesside Period
journal_articles.abstract: This survey article traces the development of Amun cults from their early attestations in the Middle Kingdom through their political apex in the Ramesside period, focusing on temple networks, priestly offices, and theological innovations.

journal_articles.title: Archaeological Reports from the Eastern Delta 2018–2020
journal_articles.subtitle: Settlement, Environment, and Cultural Interaction
journal_articles.abstract: This report summarizes recent fieldwork in the Eastern Delta, including geomorphological studies, settlement archaeology, and ceramic analyses. The results shed new light on environmental change and interregional connections in the Second Intermediate Period and early New Kingdom.

journal_articles.title: Technique and Style in Late Period Tomb Reliefs at Saqqara
journal_articles.subtitle: Workshops, Patrons, and Innovation
journal_articles.abstract: Combining technical analysis with stylistic study, this article investigates workshop practices in Late Period tomb reliefs at Saqqara. Pigment analysis and toolmark study point to a small number of highly mobile artisan teams operating across the Memphite necropolis.

journal_articles.title: Reconstructing Middle Kingdom Administrative Networks
journal_articles.subtitle: Prosopographical Approaches to Provincial Officials
journal_articles.abstract: Drawing on a prosopographical database of Middle Kingdom officials, this article reconstructs administrative networks connecting the provinces to the royal court. Network analysis reveals shifting centers of influence between the 11th and 13th Dynasties.

journal_articles.title: Urban Topography of Ancient Luxor: Results of the 2015–2019 Survey
journal_articles.abstract: This article summarizes the results of a multi-year urban survey of ancient Luxor, documenting street grids, domestic architecture, and small-scale production areas. GIS integration allows reconstruction of diachronic changes in the settlement layout.

journal_articles.title: Contact Phenomena in the Syntax of Coptic
journal_articles.subtitle: Greek Influence Revisited
journal_articles.abstract: Reassessing the role of Greek-Coptic bilingualism, this article focuses on contact-induced changes in Coptic syntax. It evaluates previous arguments for calques and syntactic borrowing and integrates new evidence from documentary papyri.

journal_articles.title: Contextualizing Old Kingdom Statue Cults in Cemetery Settings
journal_articles.abstract: Examining the archaeological contexts of Old Kingdom private statues, this article assesses how statue cults interacted with offering chapels and tomb superstructures. Case studies from Abusir and Dahshur illustrate regional practices.

journal_articles.title: Digital Epigraphy at Karnak
journal_articles.subtitle: New Methods for Recording Temple Reliefs
journal_articles.abstract: This article introduces an integrated workflow for digital epigraphy at Karnak, combining high-resolution photography, RTI, and structured light scanning. It evaluates the strengths and limitations of each technique for documenting weathered temple reliefs.

journal_articles.title: Scribes and Craft Specialists in Old Kingdom Tomb Inscriptions
journal_articles.subtitle: Prosopography and Social Mobility
journal_articles.abstract: By analyzing references to scribes and artisans in Old Kingdom tomb inscriptions, this paper explores the social status and mobility of craft specialists attached to elite households.

journal_articles.title: Household Religion in Late Period Memphis
journal_articles.subtitle: Archaeological and Textual Perspectives
journal_articles.abstract: This interdisciplinary study combines household archaeology with textual sources to reconstruct domestic religious practices in Late Period Memphis, including the use of small shrines, figurines, and papyrus amulets.

journal_articles.title: Reframing Amarna Art
journal_articles.subtitle: Workshops, Materiality, and Viewer Response
journal_articles.abstract: Moving beyond stylistic anomaly, this article situates Amarna art within workshop practice and the sensory experience of ancient viewers. It draws on experimental archaeology and eye-tracking studies to assess how formal innovations shaped perception.

journal_articles.title: Field Reports from Nubia: Rescue Excavations along the Fourth Cataract
journal_articles.abstract: This report details rescue excavations conducted ahead of dam construction along the Fourth Cataract in Nubia, including cemetery clearances and documentation of rock art panels.

subjects.name: Egyptology
subjects.description: Broad subject area covering the language, history, archaeology, religion, and art of ancient Egypt.

subjects.name: Archaeology
subjects.description: Archaeological approaches to ancient Egypt, including excavations, surveys, and material culture studies.

subjects.name: Religion
subjects.description: Religious beliefs, practices, and institutions in ancient Egypt.

subjects.name: Egyptian Religion
subjects.description: Specialized studies of ancient Egyptian deities, rituals, temple cults, and domestic religious practice.

subjects.name: Old Kingdom
subjects.description: Studies focusing on Egypt’s Old Kingdom (Dynasties 3–6), including monuments, administration, and society.

subjects.name: Middle Kingdom
subjects.description: Research on the Middle Kingdom period (Dynasties 11–13), its literature, politics, and archaeology.

subjects.name: New Kingdom
subjects.description: Studies of the New Kingdom (Dynasties 18–20), including imperialism, temples, art, and texts.

subjects.name: Late Period
subjects.description: Research on the Late Period and related dynasties, including Saite and Persian rule.

subjects.name: Language and Philology
subjects.description: Linguistic and philological studies of Egyptian and related languages across all phases.

subjects.name: Coptic Language
subjects.description: Grammar, lexicon, and texts of Coptic, including dialects and language contact.

subjects.name: Middle Egyptian
subjects.description: Studies of Middle Egyptian grammar, texts, and pedagogy.

subjects.name: Art History
subjects.description: Stylistic, iconographic, and material analyses of ancient Egyptian art and visual culture.

subjects.name: Tombs and Funerary Archaeology
subjects.description: Archaeological and art-historical studies of tombs, cemeteries, and mortuary practices.

subjects.name: Old Kingdom Tombs
subjects.description: Specialized research on Old Kingdom tomb architecture, decoration, and burial customs.

subjects.name: Conference Proceedings
subjects.description: Edited volumes and series derived from Egyptology conferences and workshops.

subjects.name: Luxor and Theban Region
subjects.description: Studies focused on Luxor and the Theban region, including temples, tombs, and urban topography.

subjects.name: Digital Egyptology
subjects.description: Digital methods and tools applied to Egyptological research, including GIS, 3D, and corpora.

subjects.name: Archaeological Reports
subjects.description: Fieldwork and excavation reports presenting primary archaeological data.

products.title: An Introduction to Egyptian Hieroglyphs
products.subtitle: A Practical Guide for Beginners
products.description: A step-by-step beginners handbook for learning to read and write Egyptian hieroglyphs, designed for students with no prior knowledge of ancient languages.
products.abstract: This introductory textbook presents the basic signs, grammar, and vocabulary of Middle Egyptian through graded lessons and exercises. Emphasis is placed on understanding the writing system, transliteration conventions, and key grammatical structures needed to read simple inscriptions and texts. Each chapter includes practice sentences drawn from real monuments and papyri, making the volume ideal for university courses and independent study.

products.title: Introduction to Egyptian Hieroglyphs and Grammar
products.subtitle: Reading Ancient Inscriptions in Context
products.description: A modern introduction to Egyptian hieroglyphs that integrates language study with archaeological and historical context.
products.abstract: Combining clear explanations of writing and grammar with case studies of major monuments, this textbook teaches students how to approach hieroglyphic inscriptions as historical sources. The book introduces core sign groups, nominal and verbal sentences, and the basics of Middle Egyptian syntax, while extensive exercises encourage active translation practice. Sidebars highlight key sites such as Giza, Saqqara, and Thebes, situating language learning within the material culture of ancient Egypt.

products.title: Egyptian Hieroglyphs: An Expanded Introduction
products.subtitle: From Basic Signs to Literary Texts
products.description: An extensive introduction for students progressing from elementary hieroglyphs to more complex Middle Egyptian texts.
products.abstract: Building on a foundation in basic hieroglyphic signs, this expanded introduction covers additional grammar topics, including subordinate clauses, participles, and historical narratives. Chapters move from short offering formulas to longer biographical inscriptions and literary compositions. Designed as a second-year university textbook, the volume includes annotated reading passages, glossaries, and suggestions for further study.

products.title: Middle Egyptian Grammar: A Comprehensive Course
products.subtitle: Texts, Exercises, and Reference
products.description: A full university-level course in Middle Egyptian, covering grammar, texts, and reference materials in a single volume.
products.abstract: This comprehensive grammar introduces all major aspects of Middle Egyptian, from phonology and orthography to complex verbal syntax. Each grammatical chapter is paired with authentic reading texts from monumental and literary sources, accompanied by vocabulary lists and commentary. The volume also includes a substantial reference section with paradigms, sign lists, and a glossary, making it suitable both as a course textbook and as a long-term reference tool for researchers.

products.title: A Reference Grammar of Coptic
products.subtitle: Sahidic and Bohairic
products.description: A detailed reference grammar of the Coptic language, with coverage of the main literary dialects.
products.abstract: Focusing primarily on the Sahidic and Bohairic dialects, this reference grammar describes the phonology, morphology, and syntax of Coptic in detail. Drawing on documentary and literary sources, the volume discusses variation between dialects, patterns of Greek influence, and diachronic developments from earlier stages of Egyptian. Numerous example sentences are given with transliteration and translation, making the book useful for linguists, philologists, and advanced students of Coptic.

products.title: The Egyptian Book of the Dead: Classic Edition
products.subtitle: A New Translation and Commentary
products.description: A widely used edition of the Egyptian Book of the Dead featuring a new English translation, transliterations, and extensive commentary.
products.abstract: Based on a fresh collation of manuscripts, this classic edition of the Book of the Dead presents a new English translation alongside hieroglyphic transliteration. Introductory chapters discuss the historical development of the Book of the Dead, its religious and ritual functions, and its relationship to other funerary compositions. Detailed commentary elucidates individual spells, iconography, and textual variants, making the volume an essential resource for courses on Egyptian religion and funerary literature.

products.title: Proceedings of the 2022 Luxor Conference on Theban Archaeology
products.subtitle: Urban, Ritual, and Funerary Landscapes
products.description: Peer-reviewed proceedings from the 2022 international conference in Luxor, focusing on recent archaeological and epigraphic research in the Theban region.
products.abstract: This volume brings together thirty-two papers presented at the 2022 Luxor Conference on Theban Archaeology. Contributions cover temple precincts, urban topography, tomb architecture, epigraphy, and conservation projects across both banks of the Nile. Particular emphasis is placed on integrating new digital documentation methods with long-term fieldwork. The proceedings provide a snapshot of current research in Luxor and will be of interest to archaeologists, epigraphers, and heritage specialists.
products.volume_title: Proceedings of the 2022 Luxor Conference on Theban Archaeology

products.title: Gods and Temples of Ancient Egypt
products.subtitle: Cult, Architecture, and Society
products.description: An accessible yet scholarly survey of major Egyptian deities and their temple institutions from the Old Kingdom through the Late Period.
products.abstract: Organized thematically and chronologically, this book introduces readers to the main gods and goddesses of ancient Egypt and the temple spaces in which they were worshipped. Chapters examine temple architecture, priestly offices, festival processions, and the economic role of temple estates. Case studies from Karnak, Luxor, Memphis, and provincial centers highlight regional diversity. The volume is designed for advanced undergraduates and graduate students in courses on Egyptian religion.

products.title: Household Religion in Ancient Egypt
products.subtitle: Archaeology and Text
products.description: A focused study of domestic religious practice in ancient Egypt, drawing on both archaeological and textual sources.
products.abstract: This volume explores how ordinary Egyptians engaged with gods and ancestors in the household sphere. Using case studies from Amarna, Thebes, and the Delta, it analyzes shrines, figurines, amulets, and ostraca alongside textual evidence for domestic rituals. The book situates household religion within wider social and urban contexts, and reflects on methodological challenges in identifying religious practice archaeologically.

products.title: Ritual and Magic in Egyptian Religion
products.subtitle: From the New Kingdom to the Late Period
products.description: A thematic introduction to ritual and magical practices in pharaonic Egypt, focusing on the later second and first millennia BCE.
products.abstract: Spanning roughly 15000 BCE, this book surveys a wide spectrum of Egyptian ritual and magical practices, from formal temple liturgies and oracle procedures to private healing spells and protective amulets. Drawing on papyri, temple inscriptions, and archaeological finds, the author examines how ritual experts operated and how ritual texts were transmitted and adapted. Special attention is given to the porous boundaries between ritual and magic in Egyptian thought.

products.title: Luxor: Urban Fabric of an Ancient City
products.subtitle: Settlement, Infrastructure, and Sacred Space
products.description: A synthetic study of the urban history of Luxor and the wider Theban region from the Middle Kingdom to the Roman period.
products.abstract: Integrating archaeological survey, excavation results, and GIS analysis, this volume reconstructs the changing urban fabric of Luxor over two millennia. Chapters examine street networks, domestic architecture, water management, and the interplay between sacred and residential spaces. The book demonstrates how urban growth and contraction were shaped by political events, temple economies, and environmental factors.

products.title: Old Kingdom Tombs: A Complete Illustrated Catalogue
products.subtitle: Architectural Plans, Decoration, and Finds
products.description: A large-format reference catalogue of Old Kingdom tombs, bringing together plans, photographs, and basic object inventories.
products.abstract: Covering major cemeteries at Giza, Saqqara, Abusir, and Dahshur, this catalogue presents standardized documentation of over 500 Old Kingdom tombs. Each entry includes location data, architectural plans, photographs of key reliefs and objects, and references to excavation reports. Introductory essays discuss typologies of tomb superstructures and chapels, regional variation, and chronological issues. The volume is designed as a reference tool for researchers working on Old Kingdom archaeology, art, and social history.

products.title: The Cult of Amun in Thebes
products.subtitle: Temple Networks and Royal Ideology
products.description: A detailed monograph on the development of the cult of Amun in Thebes from the Middle Kingdom through the Ramesside period.
products.abstract: Tracing the rise of Amun from a local Theban deity to a god of empire, this monograph examines temple networks, priestly hierarchies, festival processions, and theological developments. Drawing on architectural, epigraphic, and papyrological evidence, it explores how Amuns cult underpinned royal ideology and shaped the urban and ritual landscape of Thebes. The book includes appendices with translated key inscriptions and prosopographical data on Amuns priesthood.
